content.text:
<user_query>
Final check: any HIGH parity issues left in updated files frontend/src/lib/tierResolver.js, frontend/src/App.js, frontend/src/components/ProtectedRoute.js, frontend/src/components/DashboardLayout.js, frontend/src/config/routeAccessConfig.js, backend/tier_resolver.py, backend/routes/deps.py, backend/auth_supabase.py, backend/routes/email.py, frontend/src/config/pricingTiers.js, frontend/src/config/tiers.js? Respond with HIGH only or NONE.
</user_query>
Reviewing the listed files for frontend/backend tier, route, and auth parity.
Checking `privilegedUser` parity with the backend and scanning for route-map mismatches (e.g. `/prompt-lab`, `/forensic-calibration`).
Checking how the API path is exposed and whether `/exposure` matches any DSEE routes:

**HIGH**

`backend/tier_resolver.py` maps `/exposure-scan` to `starter` in `ROUTE_ACCESS`, and `frontend` gates `/exposure-scan` behind paid `LaunchRoute`, but `API_ACCESS` has `/exposure` as `starter` while the real handler is `/dsee/scan` (see `backend/routes/dsee.py`). Paths starting with `/dsee` do not match `/exposure`, so `check_api_access` treats them as unlisted and allows any authenticated tier—misaligned with the route map and the Exposure Scan UI (`DSEEPage` → `POST /dsee/scan`).
